vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow
vulnerability.group: Verify Permissions on Files with Local Account Information and Credentials

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting /etc/gshadow permissions to 0000 follows standard STIG hardening and does not risk SSH or console lockout. The change is limited to a single file and does not require a reboot.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential impact on tools that need to read or modify group passwords; Incorrect chmod syntax could leave the file overly permissive
